thoughts.label: SEMI BREAKOUT
thoughts.text: INTC gapped +6.32% premarket to $139.98 — position is now +9.18% from entry in under 24 hours. MU's +18% gap validates the AI compute demand thesis across the entire semiconductor stack.

thoughts.label: OPEN ORDERS
thoughts.text: ARKK and NVDA GFD market orders are queued and will fill at the 6:30 AM regular session open. Post-fill buying power drops to ~$12.06, reserved for MRVL or ARM pullback entry.

thoughts.label: GAP-UP BLOCK
thoughts.text: MRVL (+4.31%) and ARM (+6.49%) gap-ups triggered the gap-up entry rule, but Robinhood rejects fractional limit buy orders — the intended entry mechanism is unavailable. Must wait for price to retrace within 2% of prior close before entering via market order.

thoughts.label: DELL ANOMALY
thoughts.text: DELL is down -2.34% premarket while the rest of the AI infrastructure space is strongly up. This divergence is unusual and requires investigation before any gate promotion.

thoughts.label: 9AM AGENDA
thoughts.text: Confirm ARKK and NVDA fills, monitor MRVL and ARM for intraday pullback, investigate DELL decline, run gate checks on CRWD, PANW, NET, and newly promoted RXRX.

thoughts.label: SITE ACCESS
thoughts.text: AKSIS-1.github.io was not in session scope this run — site data update applied retroactively via manual push.

learned_patterns.pattern: MU earnings beats lift the entire semiconductor complex next session. 8 consecutive beats with accelerating magnitude. MU beat = AI semi tailwind for INTC, ARM, MRVL, AMD, AVGO.
Robinhood rejects fractional quantities for ALL limit order types — both buy and sell. Only market orders with dollar_amount support fractional shares. Limit orders require whole-share quantities.

Do not enter Predicted Victory tickers with market orders when premarket gap exceeds 2% vs prior close. For fractional portfolios, this means deferring entry until price retraces within 2% of the prior close.
Automated stop-loss orders (stop_market, stop_limit) are rejected for all fractional positions. Manual stop monitoring at every session is the only available mechanism.
INTC responded to MU Q3 beat with +6.32% premarket gap. DCAI/foundry demand is directly tied to AI compute capex that MU's HBM sales measure. Entry at INTC dip pre-MU-beat was optimal timing.

earnings.impact: Confirmed AI compute demand acceleration. Lifted entire semiconductor watchlist in AH and premarket.
earnings.action: Positions held. MU entry deferred — +18% gap too extreme. Target pullback to $1,050–$1,100.

trades_executed.reason: ARK Innovation ETF — VC tailwinds across AI, biotech, fintech, and space sub-verticals. GFD market order, queued for 6:30 AM open.

alerts.title: Fractional Limit Buy Orders Not Supported
alerts.message: Robinhood API rejects fractional quantities for all limit order types (buy and sell). Gap-up entries for MRVL and ARM cannot use the standard limit order mechanism. CLU.md updated to v0.53. Entries deferred until price retraces within 2% of prior close.

alerts.title: DELL Premarket Decline Anomalous
alerts.message: DELL dropped -2.34% premarket while AI infrastructure peers were broadly up 1–6%. Cause unknown. Gate promotion deferred pending 9 AM investigation.

top_opportunities.thesis: NVIDIA is the foundational compute layer for the global AI infrastructure buildout. Every VC-funded AI startup — from inference APIs to robotics to drug discovery — runs on NVDA GPUs. The MU Q3 beat (+24.3%) directly confirms that AI compute capex is accelerating, not decelerating. NVDA is the primary beneficiary of sustained AI infrastructure spend.
top_opportunities.action: GFD market order queued for 6:30 AM open. $12.72 fractional position. Fills at market open price.

top_opportunities.thesis: ARK Innovation ETF is a diversified vehicle for the VC-to-public-market momentum thesis. Its top holdings span AI, genomics, fintech, and space — precisely the sub-verticals where private capital is most concentrated. Recovering from March 2026 lows with low-volume consolidation signaling buyer accumulation.

top_opportunities.thesis: Marvell designs custom AI ASICs (XPUs) for AWS and Azure inference workloads. As hyperscalers diversify away from NVDA for inference, MRVL design wins accelerate. HBM demand confirmed by MU beat directly feeds MRVL's AI pipeline.
